name: Inherent Runge–Kutta stability
statement_text:
A general linear method (A, U, B, V ) is ‘inherently Runge–
Kutta stable’ if V is of the form (551a) and the two matrices

                  BA − XB         and         BU − XV + V X

are zero except for their first rows, where X is some matrix.

  The significance of this definition is expressed in the following.
statement_latex:
\begin{definition}
A general linear method $(A, U, B, V)$ is `inherently Runge--Kutta stable' if $V$ is of the form \eqref{eq:551a} and the two matrices
\[
BA - XB \quad \text{and} \quad BU - XV + VX
\]
are zero except for their first rows, where $X$ is some matrix.

The significance of this definition is expressed in the following.
\end{definition}
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
The definition concerns a general linear method with matrices $(A, U, B, V)$. Assume $p = q$ and $s = r = p + 1$, with $A$ diagonally implicit and $V$ having the structure
\[
V = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & v \\ 0 & \dot{V} \end{bmatrix},
\]
where $\rho(\dot{V}) = 0$. The concept of inherent Runge--Kutta stability imposes conditions on $BA - XB$ and $BU - XV + VX$.

variables.name: A
variables.definition: coefficient matrix of the general linear method, assumed to have a diagonally implicit form with λ ≥ 0 on the diagonal

variables.name: U
variables.definition: coefficient matrix of the general linear method

variables.name: B
variables.definition: coefficient matrix of the general linear method

variables.name: V
variables.definition: coefficient matrix of the general linear method, assumed to be of the form (551a)

variables.name: p
variables.definition: order of the method, assumed equal to q

variables.name: q
variables.definition: stage order, assumed equal to p

variables.name: s
variables.definition: number of stages, assumed equal to r = p + 1

variables.name: r
variables.definition: number of pieces of information passed between steps, assumed equal to s = p + 1

variables.name: V̇
variables.definition: submatrix of V such that ρ(V̇) = 0, where ρ denotes spectral radius

variables.name: X
variables.definition: some matrix (not explicitly defined in the preceding text, but introduced in the definition)

preamble: The definition concerns a general linear method represented by matrices (A, U, B, V). The method is assumed to have p = q and s = r = p + 1, with A being diagonally implicit and V having a specific block structure. The concept of inherent Runge-Kutta stability imposes conditions on the matrices BA - XB and BU - XV + VX.
introduces: inherently Runge–Kutta stable general linear method
